coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 45–50 minutes, Z2 (140–150 bpm max), rolling terrain. You're in week 23 of a 22-week plan—you've extended taper, which is fine given your travel schedule and the 81 days out. Yesterday's 12-miler at 131 avg was solid vert work; today you flush that out without adding stress. Your resting HR (63 bpm) is 2.3 bpm above your 30-day avg, which suggests residual fatigue or travel dehydration. Keep it conversational. No hills, no surges. If you feel flat, dial back to 40 minutes.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You slept 8.46 hours at 95% efficiency—excellent. That's your recovery asset this week. Eat 1.2g carb/kg body weight today (assuming ~80kg, ~96g net carbs) spread across 4–5 meals to refuel the vert damage from yesterday without overloading your gut pre-run. Prioritize low FODMAP carbs and salt; you're traveling and probably dehydrated (resting HR elevation). Post-run, hit 20–25g protein + 40g carbs within 30 minutes to lock in adaptation.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Your sleep score is your differentiator right now—protect it ruthlessly over the next 11 weeks. Keep bedtime consistent despite travel; 8+ hours is non-negotiable in taper. Recovery score is 72/100 (recovered), so you're cleared to run, but don't add mobility or strength work today. Walk 20 minutes post-run if legs feel stiff. Ice bath or cold water immersion only if you're experiencing genuine soreness; otherwise, it's noise and will disrupt sleep quality.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 11 weeks from Kranjska Gora. The hardest work is done; taper is about showing up smart, not proving fitness. Your last 48.8-mile week showed you can hold the volume. Use these 81 days to dial race strategy: pacing on descent (where 2,500m vert will hurt), nutrition windows at sparse aid stations, and mental rehearsal of the second half when quads are trashed. Run easy today and trust the block.
